title: Graphics processor register renaming mechanism
abstract: A processing apparatus is described. The apparatus includes a graphics processing unit (GPU), including a plurality of execution units to process graphics context data and a register file having a plurality of registers to store the graphics context data; and register renaming logic to facilitate dynamic renaming of the plurality of registers by logically partitioning the plurality of registers in the register file into a set of fixed registers and a set of shared registers.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A processing apparatus, comprising: a graphics processing unit (GPU), including: a plurality of execution units to process graphics context data; and a register file having a plurality of registers to store the graphics context data; and register renaming logic to facilitate dynamic renaming of the plurality of registers by logically partitioning the plurality of registers in the register file into a set of fixed registers and a set of shared registers. 2. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the set of shared registers are dynamically renamed to a shared physical register during execution of a context at an execution unit. 3. The apparatus of claim 2, wherein the GPU further comprises: a renaming table to store the renaming of each register within the set of shared registers to a shared physical register; and decoding logic to perform register renaming of operands from the set of fixed registers and operands from the set of shared registers during execution of the context via the renaming table. 4. The apparatus of claim 3, wherein the register renaming comprises: determining whether a register ID of an operand of the context is less than a starting range of the set of shared registers; and accessing the set of fixed registers to perform an operation on the operand upon determining that the operand is less than the starting range of the set of shared registers. 5. The apparatus of claim 4, wherein the register renaming further comprises: determining whether the operand is a source operand upon determining that the operand is greater than the starting range of the set of shared registers; accessing the renaming table to find a physical register corresponding to the register ID upon determining that the operand is a source operand; and fetching the operand from the set of shared registers. 6. The apparatus of claim 5, wherein the register renaming further comprises: determining whether a new shared register can be acquired upon determining that the operand is not a source operand; and 

 storing a mapping of the shared register to a physical register ID in the renaming table upon determining that the new shared register can be acquired. 7. The apparatus of claim 6, wherein the register renaming further comprises stalling the context upon determining that the new shared register can not be acquired. 8. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the register renaming logic assigns variables based on the plurality of registers during compiling of a GPU kernel. 9. The apparatus of claim 8, wherein the register renaming logic performs a register allocation algorithm to assign variables having long live ranges to the set of fixed registers and assign variables having short live ranges to the set of shared registers. 10. The apparatus of claim 9, wherein the register renaming logic facilitates the generation of spill code upon a determination that register assignments exceed the plurality of registers on the register file. 11. The apparatus of claim 8, wherein the register renaming logic provides instructions as to when a register in the set of shared registers is to be released. 12. The apparatus of claim 11, wherein a release location for a variable is marked based on information computed during the register allocation algorithm. 13. The apparatus of claim 12, wherein a location of a local variable is marked to be released after a final last use before a new full definition. 14. The apparatus of claim 13, wherein a location of a global variable is marked to be released at an immediate post-dominator in a control flow graph for basic blocks that include last uses of a previous definition. 15. A graphics processing unit (GPU) comprising: a plurality of execution units to process graphics context data; a register file having a plurality of registers to store the graphics context data; decoding logic to perform register renaming of operands from a set of fixed registers and operands from a set of shared registers during execution of a context; and a renaming table to store the renaming of each register within the set of shared registers to a shared physical register. 16. The GPU of claim 15, wherein the register renaming comprises: determining whether a register ID of an operand of the context is less than a starting range of the set of shared registers; and accessing the set of fixed registers to perform an operation on the operand upon determining that the operand is less than the starting range of the set of shared registers. 17. The GPU of claim 16, wherein the register renaming further comprises: determining whether the operand is a source operand upon determining that the operand is greater than the starting range of the set of shared registers; accessing the renaming table to find a physical register corresponding to the register ID upon determining that the operand is a source operand; and fetching the operand from the set of shared registers. 18. The GPU of claim 17, wherein the register renaming further comprises: determining whether a new shared register can be acquired upon determining that the operand is not a source operand; and storing a mapping of the shared register to a physical register ID in the renaming table upon determining that the new shared register can be acquired. 19. The GPU of claim 18, wherein the register renaming further comprises stalling the context upon determining that the new shared register can not be acquired. 20. The GPU of claim 15, further comprising register renaming logic to facilitate dynamic renaming of the plurality of registers by logically partitioning the plurality of registers in the register file into a set of fixed registers and a set of shared registers. 21. A method comprising: receiving graphics context operands at a graphics processing unit (GPU) execution unit; decoding the operands; and performing register renaming of the operands from a set of fixed registers and operands from a set of shared registers during execution of the graphics contexts. 22. The method of claim 21, wherein the register renaming comprises: determining whether a register ID of an operand of the context is less than a starting range of the set of shared registers; and 

 accessing the set of fixed registers to perform an operation on the operand upon determining that the operand is less than the starting range of the set of shared registers. 23. The method of claim 22, wherein the register renaming further comprises: determining whether the operand is a source operand upon determining that the operand is greater than the starting range of the set of shared registers; accessing the renaming table to find a physical register corresponding to the register ID upon determining that the operand is a source operand; and fetching the operand from the set of shared registers. 24. The method of claim 23, wherein the register renaming further comprises: determining whether a new shared register can be acquired upon determining that the operand is not a source operand; and storing a mapping of the shared register to a physical register ID in the renaming table upon determining that the new shared register can be acquired. 25. The method of claim 24, wherein the register renaming further comprises stalling the context upon determining that the new shared register cannot be acquired.
